user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Adatum Corporation. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 16:55 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Adatum Corporation migration architecture

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 11:16 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 10:49 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Adatum Corporation
Date: 2024-02-16
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:05:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:08:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:10:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:15:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:20:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:29:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:34:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:37:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-Q trap (Daily-Automation generating pre-packs for every meeting creates noise vs filteri); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-Q failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-Q failure mode (Daily-Automation generating pre-packs for every meeting creates noise vs filteri)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
